attacking_style: Late-race opportunist who launches solo attacks inside final 3km, typically as last survivor from breakaways rather than from bunch. Willing to gamble on short, explosive moves.
team_tactics: Minimal team support evident; operates independently in breakaways. No clear domestique setup for his moves—relies on individual timing rather than organized leadouts.
terrain_preference: Thrives in breakaway scenarios on stages where peloton control is uncertain. Final kilometer positioning suggests comfort in technical finales requiring tactical nerve over pure power.
vulnerability: Struggles with pace negotiation in small groups (evidenced by Vuelta failure with Caruso). Lacks finish speed to hold off organized peloton chases despite gaining initial gaps.
key_patterns: Launches solo attacks sub-3km when in breakaways, preferring individual gambles over cooperation (2 instances)
Consistently caught by peloton despite initial gap creation, suggesting timing or power limits (2 races)
Operates as breakaway specialist without visible team protection or organized support structure (2 instances)
form_trajectory: neutral: Both observations show similar pattern—bold attacking yielding temporary gaps but ultimate failure, suggesting consistent capability level without progression
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 3
